symptoms: Olive-brown to velvety green/black lesions on leaves and fruit
treatments: Protectant fungicides applied on schedule during wet spring periods
local_pesticides: Sulfur-based, captan, or other MoA-approved fungicides (rotate modes of action)
prevention: Resistant varieties, sanitation (remove fallen leaves), pruning for airflow
palestine_risk_season: Spring (wet periods in West Bank highlands)
local_advice: Common in apple orchards; monitor humidity. Cultural practices key in resource-limited settings.
sources: Plantwise Knowledge Bank; regional Levant pome fruit research (Jordan/Lebanon analogs)
